summary: No-ship: the plan’s load-bearing characterization lock overclaims coverage. Simpler alternative: I do not see a materially simpler refactor than the narrowed kernel; fix the lock before execution.

findings.title: Behavior-lock coverage is materially overstated
findings.body: The plan says B1-B10 are already covered by the five committed Bats suites, but those suites do not pin several rows that the refactor is about to touch. Examples: poll-ghcr has no baseline test for malformed inline pins or the exact B2 refuse strings; bump inline mode checks only the parsed image value, not the B6 line comment or byte-identical YAML output; create-app has no malformed tag/digest cases for B9; and B4/B8 exact stderr/log behavior is only partially checked. A competent engineer could follow this plan, change user-visible refuse messages or YAML/comment output, and still get the stated lock command green.
findings.recommendation: Before starting R-1, add baseline characterization cases for every Behavior Contract row at the stable CLI/file-output seams: exact poll refuse reasons for invalid tag/inline pin/repo mismatch/parse failure, exact bump exit/message cases, full-file or targeted comment assertions for inline YAML output, create-app invalid tag/digest exit text, digest-exporter log text, and boundary accept/reject cases for tag/digest/inline regexes. Then update characterization-lock.json with the new green case count.

next_steps: Backfill the missing baseline tests before any extraction commit.
Keep the five existing suites unmodified except for additive characterization cases tied to the Behavior Contract.
